articles.title: J&K announces summer vacation for Jammu schools from June 1 amid intense heatwave - DD News
articles.full_text:
The Jammu and Kashmir government on Saturday announced summer vacation for schools in the Jammu division in view of the prevailing heatwave conditions in the region.
Jammu and Kashmir education minister Sakina Itoo said on X that summer vacations for schools falling under the Summer Zone of Jammu Division will begin from June 1 for primary classes and from June 8 for students of Classes 6 to 12.
“Keeping in view the prevailing weather conditions, summer vacations for schools falling under Summer Zone of Jammu Division shall commence with effect from June 1 for Primary Classes and with effect from June 8 for students of Classes 6 to 12. Wishing all students a safe and enjoyable vacation,” she posted.
Meanwhile, the Jammu Municipal Corporation (JMC) issued a heatwave advisory after temperatures in the city were forecast to touch 45 degrees Celsius. Authorities urged people, especially children and elderly citizens, to remain indoors and avoid unnecessary movement during peak afternoon hours.
The civic body said prolonged exposure to heat could lead to dehydration, exhaustion and heat-related illnesses. Water sprinkling vehicles have been deployed in several parts of the city to reduce surface temperatures and provide temporary relief to commuters and pedestrians.
The JMC advised residents to stay hydrated, wear light cotton clothes, use umbrellas or head coverings while stepping outdoors and avoid direct exposure to sunlight. People were also asked to keep homes properly ventilated and consume adequate fluids during the extreme weather conditions.
Special caution has been advised for children, elderly citizens and those suffering from health complications. Authorities also warned residents against leaving children or elderly persons inside parked vehicles, saying enclosed vehicles can become dangerously hot within minutes.
Officials said field workers and sanitation staff working outdoors have been instructed to take regular hydration breaks and follow precautionary measures.
According to data released by the Meteorological Centre Srinagar, Srinagar recorded a maximum temperature of 23.9 degrees Celsius on Saturday, 1.5 degrees below normal for this time of the year, while Jammu recorded 39.9 degrees Celsius, 1.5 degrees above normal.
-IANS

articles.title: Editorial | Water Security Matters Now - The Global Kashmir
articles.full_text:
The ongoing heatwave across Jammu and Kashmir has once again highlighted the importance of reliable water supply. As temperatures continue to remain above normal in many areas, the demand for water has increased significantly. Households, schools, hospitals, businesses and farms all depend on uninterrupted access to this essential resource. Any disruption during periods of extreme heat quickly affects daily life and public well being.
Reports of water shortages from different areas have caused concern among residents. While the situation varies from place to place, the larger message is clear. Water management is becoming an increasingly important challenge that requires sustained attention. Rising temperatures, changing weather patterns and growing demand are placing additional pressure on available resources.
Summer months traditionally bring higher water consumption. People require more water for drinking, cooking and sanitation. Livestock and agriculture also depend heavily on adequate supplies. During heatwaves, this demand rises even further. Water supply systems therefore face greater pressure at a time when natural sources may also experience seasonal stress.
Jammu and Kashmir has long been known for its rivers, streams, lakes and springs. These resources remain among the region’s greatest strengths. However, the presence of water sources alone does not guarantee smooth supply to every household. Effective storage, distribution and conservation are equally important. Modern infrastructure and careful planning are necessary to ensure that water reaches communities when it is needed most.
The present conditions offer an opportunity to strengthen long term preparedness. Authorities at different levels have already taken steps to address seasonal challenges through maintenance work, supply management and public outreach. Such efforts deserve continued support. At the same time, regular assessment of infrastructure can help identify areas where improvements may be required. Reducing leakages, upgrading pipelines and improving storage facilities can enhance efficiency and reliability.
Public participation is equally important. Responsible use of water can help reduce unnecessary wastage, especially during periods of high demand. Simple practices such as repairing leaking taps, avoiding excessive use and storing water carefully can contribute to conservation efforts. When communities and institutions work together, the impact can be significant.
Environmental protection also plays a key role in water security. Springs, wetlands and natural recharge areas support the broader ecosystem and contribute to sustainable water availability. Protecting these resources benefits both present and future generations. Community awareness programmes and local conservation initiatives can help strengthen this effort.
Climate experts have repeatedly pointed to the increasing frequency of extreme weather events across many parts of the world. Heatwaves, irregular rainfall and changing seasonal patterns require adaptive planning. Water management strategies must therefore focus not only on current needs but also on future challenges. Building resilience today will help reduce pressure during similar situations in the years ahead.
Educational institutions can also contribute by promoting awareness about conservation and sustainable practices. Young people often play an important role in encouraging responsible behaviour within families and communities. Public awareness campaigns can reinforce the message that every drop of water has value.
The current heatwave serves as a reminder of the importance of preparedness, coordination and responsible resource management. Reliable water supply remains essential for public health, economic activity and overall quality of life. Continued efforts to improve infrastructure, encourage conservation and protect natural resources will strengthen the region’s ability to meet future challenges.
Water is a shared resource and a shared responsibility. Ensuring its sustainable use requires cooperation among authorities, communities and individuals alike. With thoughtful planning and collective effort, Jammu and Kashmir can continue to safeguard this vital resource and build greater resilience against the challenges posed by rising temperatures and changing climate conditions.

articles.title: Jammu News: सांबा में 8 जुलाई तक भारी बारिश की संभावना, जिला प्रशासन ने जारी की एडवाइजरी - Amar Ujala

Jammu News: सांबा में 8 जुलाई तक भारी बारिश की संभावना, जिला प्रशासन ने जारी की एडवाइजरी
विज्ञापन
खबरें लगातार पढ़ने के लिए अमर उजाला एप डाउनलोड करें
या
वेबसाइट पर पढ़ना जारी रखने के लिए वीडियो विज्ञापन देखें
अगर आपके पास प्रीमियम मेंबरशिप है तो
सांबा। भारतीय मौसम विज्ञान विभाग की तरफ से 6 से 8 जुलाई के दौरान जिला सांबा में हल्की से मध्यम बारिश, गरज-चमक तथा कुछ स्थानों पर भारी वर्षा की संभावना जताई गई है। इसे देखते हुए जिला प्रशासन सांबा ने लोगों के लिए मौसम संबंधी परामर्श (एडवाइजरी) जारी करते हुए सतर्क रहने और आवश्यक सावधानियां बरतने की अपील की है।
जिला प्रशासन ने नागरिकों से नदियों, नालों, खड्डों और जलभराव वाले क्षेत्रों को पार करने से बचने की सलाह दी है। साथ ही जलमग्न सड़कों, क्षतिग्रस्त पुलों अथवा तेज बहाव वाले स्थानों पर वाहन न चलाने की हिदायत दी गई है।
एडवाइजरी में अभिभावकों से बच्चों को नदियों, नालों और बाढ़ संभावित क्षेत्रों से दूर रखने का आग्रह किया गया है। निचले इलाकों में रहने वाले लोगों को सतर्क रहने तथा आवश्यकता पड़ने पर सुरक्षित स्थानों पर जाने के लिए तैयार रहने को कहा गया है। प्रशासन ने लोगों से केवल आधिकारिक मौसम अपडेट और जिला प्रशासन द्वारा जारी निर्देशों का ही पालन करने की अपील की है।
विज्ञापन
विज्ञापन
